question: [File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/Transaction.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: isNew()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that isNew() always returns true to cause duplicate Transaction records to be inserted with the same consensusTimestamp primary key, bypassing Hibernate's existence check and corrupting the transaction ledger?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/Transaction.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: toTransactionHash()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction with a crafted transactionHash byte[] that collides with an existing hash in the sharded transaction_hash table, causing toTransactionHash() to produce a record that overwrites or shadows a legitimate transaction's hash lookup entry?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/Transaction.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: toTransactionHash()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction where transactionHash is null, causing toTransactionHash() to invoke payerAccountId.getId() on a null hash and produce a TransactionHash with a null hash field, leading to a NullPointerException or silent data corruption in the hash index?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/Transaction.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: toTransactionHash()] Can an unprivileged external user tamper with the payerAccountId field before toTransactionHash() is called, causing the resulting TransactionHash to map the transaction hash to a different payer account, allowing the attacker to misattribute fees or impersonate another account in hash-based lookups?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/Transaction.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: addInnerTransaction()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a transaction with type set to the integer value of ATOMIC_BATCH (74) without actually being an atomic batch, then call addInnerTransaction() to inject arbitrary payer_account_id and valid_start_ns pairs into the innerTransactions list, corrupting the batch membership record?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/Transaction.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: addInnerTransaction()] Can an unprivileged external user submit an inner transaction with a null payerAccountId, causing addInnerTransaction() to call transaction.getPayerAccountId().getId() and throw a NullPointerException that disrupts the processing pipeline for the entire atomic batch?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/Transaction.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: addInnerTransaction()] Can an unprivileged external user submit an inner transaction with a null validStartNs, causing addInnerTransaction() to add a null Long into the innerTransactions list, which when serialized via ListToStringSerializer produces malformed output that breaks downstream consumers parsing the inner transaction list?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/Transaction.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: addInnerTransaction()] Can an unprivileged external user repeatedly call addInnerTransaction() on a single ATOMIC_BATCH transaction with thousands of crafted inner transactions, causing unbounded growth of the innerTransactions ArrayList and triggering an out-of-memory condition in the importer service?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/Transaction.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: addItemizedTransfer()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction that triggers addItemizedTransfer() with a crafted ItemizedTransfer containing a null entityId, causing the JSON serialization via ObjectToStringSerializer to produce malformed JSON stored in the itemizedTransfer column, corrupting transfer records for that transaction?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/Transaction.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: addItemizedTransfer()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction that causes addItemizedTransfer() to be called an unbounded number of times, growing the itemizedTransfer list without limit and causing excessive JSON payload sizes stored in the database, leading to storage exhaustion or query performance degradation?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/Transaction.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: addItemizedTransfer()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a transaction where ItemizedTransfer.amount is set to Long.MIN_VALUE or Long.MAX_VALUE, causing addItemizedTransfer() to store an extreme value in the itemizedTransfer JSON column that overflows balance calculations in downstream REST API consumers?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/Transaction.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: addNftTransfer()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction that triggers addNftTransfer() with a crafted NftTransfer containing a null sender or receiver EntityId, causing the JSON serialization to produce malformed JSON in the nftTransfer column and corrupting NFT ownership records?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/Transaction.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: addNftTransfer()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction that causes addNftTransfer() to be called with duplicate NftTransfer entries for the same serial number, causing the nftTransfer JSON list to contain conflicting ownership records that mislead NFT ownership queries?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/Transaction.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: addNftTransfer()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction that triggers addNftTransfer() an unbounded number of times, growing the nftTransfer ArrayList without limit and causing the JSON stored in the nftTransfer column to exceed database field size limits, resulting in a truncation or insert failure that drops the entire transaction record?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/Transaction.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Field: transactionHash] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction with a transactionHash byte[] of length other than 32 or 48 bytes, causing TransactionHash.setHash() to silently discard the hash (storing null), making the transaction invisible to all hash-based lookups via get_transaction_info_by_hash()?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/Transaction.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Field: transactionHash] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction with a 48-byte transactionHash where the first 32 bytes are identical to a legitimate transaction's hash, causing calculateV1Shard() to route the tampered hash to the same shard and the hash lookup to return both transactions, confusing clients about which transaction is authoritative?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/Transaction.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Field: transactionHash] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction with a transactionHash whose first byte is crafted to map to a specific shard via calculateV1Shard() (mod 32), concentrating all tampered transactions into one shard partition and causing a targeted denial-of-service on that shard's lookup performance?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/Transaction.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Field: consensusTimestamp] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction with a consensusTimestamp value of 0 or Long.MIN_VALUE, causing getId() to return an invalid primary key that conflicts with the database schema constraints and disrupts the ordering of all subsequent transaction queries?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/Transaction.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Field: consensusTimestamp] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction with a consensusTimestamp that duplicates an existing record's timestamp, and since isNew() always returns true (skipping Hibernate's existence check), cause a duplicate key violation that rolls back the entire batch of transactions being processed, resulting in data loss for legitimate transactions in the same batch?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/Transaction.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Field: type] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction with a type integer value that does not correspond to any known TransactionType enum entry, causing TransactionType.of() to return UNKNOWN(-1) and downstream logic that branches on transaction type to silently misclassify the transaction, bypassing type-specific validation?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/Transaction.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Field: type] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction with type set to 74 (ATOMIC_BATCH) when the transaction is not actually an atomic batch, causing addInnerTransaction() to succeed without throwing IllegalStateException and allowing arbitrary innerTransactions data to be injected into the record?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/Transaction.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Field: type] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction with a negative type integer (e.g., -1 for UNKNOWN), causing the EntityOperation lookup to return NONE and suppressing entity creation/update/delete side effects that should have been triggered, leaving the entity state inconsistent with the transaction record?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/Transaction.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Field: result] Can an unprivileged external user tamper with the result integer field of a submitted transaction to set it to a success code (e.g., 22 for SUCCESS) when the actual transaction failed, causing the mirror node to record a false success status that misleads clients querying transaction outcomes?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/Transaction.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Field: result] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction with a result value of null (since it is Integer, not int), causing downstream code that calls getResult() and unboxes it to throw a NullPointerException, disrupting the transaction processing pipeline?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/Transaction.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Field: payerAccountId] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction with a payerAccountId EntityId that encodes a non-existent account, causing toTransactionHash() to store a payer_account_id in the transaction_hash table that does not correspond to any real account, corrupting fee attribution and account-based transaction lookups?
